title: Air-Fried Herbs Vietnamese Stir-fry
description: This romantic and dense herbal dish features sweet chilli sauce for a unique twist on traditional Vietnamese stir-fries, offering a delightful low-calorie option.
instructions: Wash and pat dry the mixed herbs, cherry tomatoes, and shallots.
Slice the shallots thinly and chop the mixed herbs roughly.
Heat a non-stick air fryer to 180°C (350°F).
Drizzle olive oil over the sliced shallots in the air fryer basket. Season with salt and pepper, then arrange them evenly on the tray.
Fry the shallots for about 6 minutes or until crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
In a small bowl, mix together sweet chili sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame seeds to create the dipping sauce.
Wipe out any excess oil from the air fryer basket. Arrange cherry tomatoes on top of the fried shallots in the basket.
Drizzle the remaining sweet chili mixture evenly over the tomato slices. Season with salt and pepper as desired.
Place the tray in the preheated air fryer and cook for about 5 minutes or until the tomatoes are slightly softened.
Gently toss the mixed herbs into the fried shallots, ensuring they adhere to them well.
Serve immediately by topping each dish of shallot and tomato with a few of the mixed herb garnish. Serve alongside the dipping sauce for extra flavor.
